channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: 4 Trump rivals that Biden didn't pardon
item.description: While President Joe Biden issued many pardons before leaving office, he did not pardon various individuals Trump has blasted who were connected to cases against the Republican figure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oath Keepers' Rhodes and Proud Boys' Tarrio released from prison after Trump Jan. 6 clemency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former Proud Boys leader Enrique Tarrio and Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes have been released from prison after their lengthy sentences for seditious conspiracy convictions in the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 Americans released in exchange for Taliban prisoner
item.description: Two Americans have been freed in a prisoner swap between the U.S. and Afghanistan’s Taliban in exchange for a Taliban figure, officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate panel advances Noem nomination for Homeland Security secretary
item.description: A Senate committee cleared President Trump's nominee to lead the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) with a bipartisan vote, paving the way for a Senate floor vote. The Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee advanced South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem (R) by a 13-2 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is withdrawing from the Paris Agreement (again), reversing U.S. climate policy
item.description: President Donald Trump issued a barrage of orders Monday targeting Biden-era initiatives on global warming. The decisions come on the heels of the hottest year ever recorded.
item.dc:creator: Nate Perez and Rachel Waldholz, MPR News

item.title: Coast Guard commandant terminated over border lapses, recruitment, DEI focus: official
item.description: The commandant of the U.S. Coast Guard has been terminated amid border, recruitment concerns, "erosion of trust," a senior DHS official confirmed to Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump revokes security clearances of 51 intel officials who signed discredited Hunter Biden laptop letter
item.description: The officials previously wrote a letter claiming that Hunter Biden's laptop had "all the classic earmarks of a Russian information operation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nancy Pelosi slams Trump’s ‘shameful’ pardons of Jan 6 defendants
item.description: Pelosi calls to remember law enforcement 'heroes' who 'ensured that democracy survived'
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signs executive order to pause TikTok ban, provide immunity to tech firms
item.description: The order follows TikTok going dark for about 14 hours after the Supreme Court upheld a law prohibiting the service from operating in the U.S. unless it breaks away from its parent company in China.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs executive order directing US withdrawal from the Paris climate agreement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order Monday directing the United States to again withdraw from the landmark Paris climate agreement, dealing a blow to worldwide efforts to combat global warming and once again distancing the U.S. from its closest allies.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY and SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Billionaires, tech titans, presidents: A guide to who stood where at Trump's inauguration
item.description: The crowded scene in the Capitol Rotunda on Inauguration Day featured four of the world’s five wealthiest men, five U.S. presidents, influential sporting figures and two foreign leaders with prime seats on the dais.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon And Alex Connor, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ratcliffe moves closer to CIA chief confirmation
item.description: John Ratcliffe took a step closer to being confirmed to lead the CIA on Monday as the Senate Intelligence Committee advanced his nomination. The panel advanced his nomination 14 to 3 shortly after President Trump was sworn into office. All three "no" votes were cast by Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Senate panel advances Defense secretary nominee Hegseth on party-line vote
item.description: The Senate Armed Services Committee on Monday voted to advance Pete Hegseth, President Trump’s nominee to serve as secretary of Defense, to the floor. The 14-13 vote was strictly along party lines.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk already has a White House email address
item.description: "It seems to confirm he's going to have an EEOB office," the source said, using the acronym for Eisenhower Executive Office Building.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, NewsNation

item.title: Texas could join Louisiana with a law to require the Ten Commandments in classrooms
item.description: Similar proposals are in multiple states after a court ruling in 2022, though opponents say the move would impose one religious view on people with different religious traditions
item.dc:creator: Andrew Schneider / Houston Public Media, MPR News

item.title: Trump returning to power after unprecedented comeback, emboldened to reshape American institutions
item.description: Donald Trump, who overcame impeachments, criminal indictments and a pair of assassination attempts to win another term in the White House, will be sworn in as the 47th president on Monday, taking charge as Republicans assume unified control of Washington and set out to reshape the country's institut
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump sets out to erase Biden's legacy with pardons and orders immediately after taking office
item.description: Donald Trump has begun erasing Joe Biden’s legacy after taking office as the nation’s 47th president.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Chris Megerian And Michelle Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden spends his final full day in office in South Carolina. It helped him become president
item.description: Joe Biden is spending his final full day in office Sunday in South Carolina, a state that holds special meaning after his commanding win in the 2020 Democratic primary there set him up to achieve his life's goal of being elected president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Special session rebuttal on illegal immigration continues in Florida
item.description: The governor gave his order Monday with House and Senate leadership pushing back, and that's not the only rebuttal we've seen in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawler: Trump has right to revoke congestion pricing approval
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Congestion pricing began in New York City earlier this month. Under congestion pricing, drivers are charged a $9 toll during peak hours to enter Manhattan’s central business district at and below 60th Street. U.S. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Local reactions mixed as Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal moves forward
item.description: The Middle East and the world are celebrating the Israel-Hamas ceasefire as a possible step toward peace, but many are still cautious about the deal.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5

item.title: Tampa Bay native Pam Bondi goes through Senate confirmation hearings to become U.S. Attorney General
item.description: Pam Bondi grew up in Temple Terrace, she worked in the Hillsborough County State Attorney's office as a prosecutor, and she served two terms as the elected Florida State Attorney General.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Patterson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Blinken makes the case for post-war reconstruction, security and governance of Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken made a last-minute case Tuesday for a plan for the post-war reconstruction and governance of Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas appears tantalizingly close to completion.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, Fox 5
